questions.q: Maulana Abul Kalam Azad became independent India's first holder of which ministerial office?
questions.explanation: Azad became independent India's first Education Minister.

questions.q: Azad was a strong advocate of which ideal during the freedom struggle?
options: Hindu-Muslim unity and a united India
questions.explanation: Azad was a firm advocate of Hindu-Muslim unity and opposed the partition of India.

questions.q: Azad served as Congress president during which major movement of the 1940s?
questions.explanation: Azad served as Congress president during the Quit India years of the early 1940s.

questions.q: Besides being a politician, Azad was renowned as a?
questions.explanation: Azad was a distinguished scholar and journalist as well as a senior political leader.

questions.q: Maulana Azad was a senior leader of which political organization?
questions.explanation: Azad was a senior leader of the Indian National Congress.

questions.q: Azad firmly opposed which 1947 outcome of the freedom struggle?
options: The partition of India and creation of Pakistan
questions.explanation: Azad strongly opposed the partition of India and the creation of Pakistan, believing in a united nation.

questions.q: The pen name 'Azad', which became part of his name, means what?
questions.explanation: Abul Kalam adopted the pen name 'Azad', meaning 'free', reflecting his independent spirit.

questions.q: Azad's birthday, 11 November, is commemorated in India as which day?
questions.explanation: Azad's birth anniversary, 11 November, is observed as National Education Day in honour of his work as Education Minister.

questions.q: Azad began his career writing on political and religious themes. He was therefore closely connected with which field?
questions.explanation: Azad was deeply connected with journalism, editing influential Urdu newspapers.

questions.q: In which language did Azad chiefly write his celebrated essays and journalism?
questions.explanation: Azad wrote chiefly in Urdu, in which he was a master of prose and oratory.

questions.q: What was the name of the influential Urdu weekly Azad launched in 1912?
questions.explanation: Azad founded the Urdu weekly Al-Hilal in 1912, which spread nationalist and anti-colonial ideas.

questions.q: After Al-Hilal was banned, which other journal did Azad start?
questions.explanation: When Al-Hilal was suppressed, Azad started another journal called Al-Balagh.

questions.q: In which city was Maulana Azad born in 1888?
questions.explanation: Azad was born in Mecca in 1888; his family later settled in Calcutta.

questions.q: Azad first became president of the Indian National Congress in 1923 at a remarkably young age. In which decade of his life was he?
questions.explanation: Azad first became Congress president in 1923 at the age of about 35, making him one of the youngest to hold the office.

questions.q: Azad's notable memoir of the freedom struggle and partition years was titled?
questions.explanation: Azad's memoir 'India Wins Freedom' recounts his account of the independence and partition period.

questions.q: Azad supported which mass movement in 1920 that linked the Khilafat cause with the national struggle?
questions.explanation: Azad actively supported the Non-Cooperation Movement and the linked Khilafat cause in 1920.

questions.q: As Education Minister, Azad is credited with helping establish which set of institutions?
options: The early IITs and the framework for the University Grants Commission
questions.explanation: As Education Minister, Azad oversaw the founding of institutions including the early IITs and the framework that led to the University Grants Commission.

questions.q: Azad presided over the important 1940 Congress session held at which town?
questions.explanation: Azad presided over the Ramgarh session of the Congress in 1940 and continued as president through the Quit India years.

questions.q: What was Azad's actual given name before he adopted his pen name?
questions.explanation: Azad's given name was Abul Kalam Ghulam Muhiyuddin Ahmed, to which he added the pen name Azad.

questions.q: During the Quit India Movement of 1942, what happened to Azad?
options: He was arrested and imprisoned with other Congress leaders
questions.explanation: As Congress president, Azad was arrested and imprisoned along with the other leaders when Quit India was launched in 1942.

questions.q: Azad was posthumously awarded India's highest civilian honour. Which award was it?
questions.explanation: Azad was posthumously awarded the Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian honour.

questions.q: Azad argued that Islam itself did not require Muslims to seek a separate nation. He thus opposed the demand for?
questions.explanation: Azad maintained that Islam did not call for a separate Muslim state and consistently opposed the demand for Pakistan.

questions.q: Azad's multi-volume Urdu commentary on the Quran was titled?
questions.explanation: Azad wrote 'Tarjuman al-Quran', a noted Urdu translation and commentary on the Quran.

questions.q: Azad's collection of personal letters written from Ahmednagar Fort prison was published under which title?
questions.explanation: Azad's reflective prison letters were published as 'Ghubar-e-Khatir', regarded as a masterpiece of Urdu prose.

questions.q: Azad held the Congress presidency continuously through the wartime years from 1940. Until which year did this long tenure last?
questions.explanation: Azad served continuously as Congress president from 1940 until 1946, an unusually long tenure spanning the Quit India and negotiation years.

questions.q: Azad was a key Congress negotiator in talks with which 1946 British mission that proposed a framework to transfer power?
questions.explanation: As Congress president, Azad was a central figure in the negotiations with the Cabinet Mission of 1946.

questions.q: Azad's early ideological outlook was shaped by Pan-Islamic thought, but he later moved decisively toward which broader vision?
questions.explanation: Though influenced early by Pan-Islamism, Azad evolved into a champion of composite Indian nationalism and a united India.

questions.q: Some pages of Azad's memoir 'India Wins Freedom' were sealed and released only thirty years after his death. What was their significance?
options: They contained candid observations on contemporaries and partition
They were written in a secret code
questions.explanation: Thirty pages of 'India Wins Freedom' were withheld at Azad's instruction and released only thirty years later, containing candid views on partition and leading contemporaries.

questions.q: In his early political life, Azad was associated with revolutionary circles in Bengal before fully committing to which method of struggle?
questions.explanation: Azad had early contact with Bengal's revolutionaries but committed himself to constitutional and mass nationalist methods within the Congress.

questions.q: In which year did Maulana Azad die, while serving in independent India's government?
questions.explanation: Azad died in 1958 while still serving as Education Minister in independent India.
